content: I'm so excited for my school's upcoming ski trip. I've been reading a lot of books on the subject to get some inspiration for our trip.
That sounds like a great trip. What drew you to the topic of skiing?

I've always been fascinated by winter sports and the outdoors. I've also been interested in astronomy for a few years now, and I see a lot of similarities between the two in terms of the beauty and wonder of the natural world.
That's really interesting. I wouldn't have thought to compare skiing and astronomy. Are you planning on featuring any notable locations in your trip?
Yeah, I'm actually planning to take the kids to a resort in the mountains near Pittsburgh, where I live. I love the scenery and the challenge of teaching them how to ski.
That sounds like a great idea. The mountains can be a really beautiful and dramatic backdrop for a ski trip. What made you decide to become an elementary school principal?

I've always been interested in education and working with kids, and I found that being a principal was a great way to combine those interests. I actually got my start in the field about 20 years ago, when I was working as a teacher.
That's great. It's always good to have a strong foundation in the field. What do you like to do in your free time besides skiing and astronomy?
I also enjoy speedcubing and reading sci-fi novels. I find that it helps me relax and think strategically.

That makes sense. It's great that you have so many hobbies. Do you find that your work as an elementary school principal gives you a lot of opportunities to make a positive impact on your students' lives?

Yeah, I do. I've been able to help a lot of kids over the years, and it's really rewarding to see them learn and grow. I've also been able to work with some great colleagues, including my vice principal, Sarah.
That's really great. It's always good to have a supportive team. Do you have any fun plans for the rest of the year?
Yeah, I'm actually planning a trip to the beach in July to relax and unwind. I'm looking forward to spending some time with my family and getting some rest.
That sounds like a great idea. You deserve a break after a long school year. Good luck with your trip and your school year.
